records.ety: [OE. brewen, AS. brewan; akin to D. brouwen, OHG. priuwan, MHG. briuwen, brūwen, G. brauen, Icel. brugga, Sw. brygga, Dan. brygge, and perh. to L. defrutum must boiled down, Gr. bry^ton (for fry^ton?) a kind of beer. The original meaning seems to have been to prepare by heat. √93. Cf. Broth, Bread.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Brewed (brd); p. pr. & vb. n. Brewing.]

defs: To boil or seethe; to cook.
To prepare, as beer or other liquor, from malt and hops, or from other materials, by steeping, boiling, and fermentation.
To prepare by steeping and mingling; to concoct.
quotes: Go, brew me a pottle of sack finely.

defs: To foment or prepare, as by brewing; to contrive; to plot; to concoct; to hatch; as, to brew mischief.
quotes: Hence with thy brewed enchantments, foul deceiver!

defs: To attend to the business, or go through the processes, of brewing or making beer.
quotes: I wash, wring, brew, bake, scour.

defs: To be in a state of preparation; to be mixing, forming, or gathering; as, a storm brews in the west.
quotes: There is some ill a-brewing towards my rest.

defs: The mixture formed by brewing; that which is brewed.
